results.title: Evaluation best practices | OpenAI API
excerpts: Quantitative evals provide a numerical score you can use to filter and rank results. They provide useful benchmarks for automated regression testing. Examples: Exact match, string match, ROUGE/BLEU scoring, function call accuracy, executable evals (executed to assess functionality or behavior—e.g., text2sql)

results.title: HELM Lite: Lightweight and Broad Capabilities Evaluation
excerpts: The original goal of HELM was to holistically evaluate all the language models we had access to on a set of representative scenarios (capturing language abilities, reasoning abilities, knowledge, etc.) and multiple metrics (accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency).

results.title: GitHub - stanford-crfm/helm: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models (HELM) is an open source Python framework created by the Center for Research on Foundation Models (CRFM) at Stanford for holistic, reproducible and transparent evaluation of foundation models, including large language models (LLMs) and multimodal models. · GitHub

results.title: BERTScore For LLM Evaluation - Comet
excerpts: To do this, we’ll implement BERTScore in Opik, Comet’s open-source LLM evaluation framework. We’ll leverage Hugging Face’s evaluate implementation of BERTScore, modifying it slightly to create a custom Opik metric with a score method that returns a ScoreResult object:
